After committing, if the branch is checked out it runs `git checkout HEAD --` on the committed `impl_context_paths` via `run_subprocess_with_context()` to bring the index back in sync. 104→ 105→### Implementation 106→ 107→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/abc.py:282-294 --> 108→ 109→See `update_local_ref()` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/abc.py` (lines 282-294). 110→ 111→### Usage in Trunk Sync 112→ 113→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_helpers.py, ensure_trunk_synced --> 114→ 115→`ensure_trunk_synced()` in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_helpers.py` (lines 26-88) uses this pattern to sync the local trunk branch to match remote without a checkout: 116→ 117→1. Fetches the remote branch 118→2. Compares local and remote SHAs 119→3. Checks merge-base to determine sync direction

44→## Branch Handling 45→ 46→Uses the same [checked-out branch handling pattern](../architecture/checked-out-branch-handling.md) as regular dispatch — detects whether the target branch is checked out in a worktree and uses `update_local_ref()` instead of `create_branch()` when needed. 47→ 48→## Related Topics 49→ 50→- [Checked-Out Branch Handling](../architecture/checked-out-branch-handling.md) — shared branch sync pattern 51→- [Planned PR Lifecycle](planned-pr-lifecycle.md) — full plan lifecycle documentation 52→

session.assistant: Now update `rebase-confirmation-workflow.md` to clarify that the entire rebase state validation is skipped (not just the tracking check):
session.tool_result:
26→### 2. Git Rebase Path 27→ 28→When Graphite is not enabled and no rebase is in progress, requires `--target` flag. Runs `git rebase` onto target. If rebase succeeds, returns. If conflicts remain, falls through to confirmation. 29→ 30→### 3. In-Progress Rebase Path 31→ 32→Detects an existing rebase in progress via `is_rebase_in_progress()`. Displays "Rebase in progress" message and falls through to confirmation. 33→ 34→## Confirmation Pattern 35→ 36→After any path produces conflicts, the command: 37→ 38→1. Retrieves conflicted files via `ctx.git.status.get_conflicted_files(cwd)` 39→2. Displays each file with red bold styling 40→3. Prompts: `click.confirm("Launch Claude to resolve conflicts?", default=True)`

session.assistant:
The plan says: clarify that entire rebase state validation is skipped when `is_rebase_in_progress()` is true. Looking at the `resolve_conflicts_cmd.py`, `is_rebase_in_progress()` is used as a precondition check — if it returns `false`, the command errors out entirely. The "In-Progress Rebase Path" description needs clarification about this guard being a gate (not a bypass of other validation). Let me check what the plan specifically says:
The plan says: "Clarify: entire rebase state validation is skipped (not just tracking check)... Reason: in-progress rebases may have detached HEAD (different git state model)"
This is about the `erk pr rebase` command (not `resolve-conflicts`). But there's no `rebase_cmd.py`. Let me check the current command structure:
session.tool_result: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/capabilities/workflows/pr_rebase.py /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/resolve_conflicts_cmd.py /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/checkout_cmd.py /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/diverge_fix_cmd.py /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/setup_impl_from_pr.py /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/rebase_with_conflict_resolution.py

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3 2→"""Rebase onto target branch and resolve conflicts with Claude. 3→ 4→This command handles merge conflicts during CI workflows by: 5→1. Fetching the target branch (trunk or parent branch for stacked PRs) 6→2. Checking if the current branch is behind 7→3. Starting a rebase 8→4. Using Claude to resolve any conflicts 9→5. Force pushing after successful rebase 10→6. Generating an intelligent summary of the rebase operation 11→ 12→Usage: 13→ erk exec rebase-with-conflict-resolution \ 14→ --target-branch master \ 15→ --branch-name feature-branch \ 16→ --model claude-sonnet-4-5 17→ 18→Output: 19→ Natural language summary suitable for PR comments. 20→ 21→Exit Codes: 22→ 0: Success (rebase completed and pushed, or already up-to-date) 23→ 1: Error (conflict resolution failed after max attempts) 24→ 25→Examples: 26→ $ erk exec rebase-with-conflict-resolution --target-branch main --branch-name my-feature 27→ Rebased `my-feature` onto `main`, resolving 3 commits behind. 28→ Resolved merge conflicts in 2 files: 29→ - src/config.py: Merged new logging settings with updated timeout values 30→ - tests/test_api.py: Combined new test cases with fixture updates
... [25 lines omitted] ...
46→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError
261: while git.rebase.is_rebase_in_progress(cwd) and attempt < max_attempts: 274: if git.rebase.is_rebase_in_progress(cwd):
session.assistant:
The plan references `rebase_cmd.py:97` for `is_rebase_in_progress` but this file doesn't exist. The actual command is `resolve_conflicts_cmd.py`. Looking at the doc — the current description says "Detects an existing rebase in progress via `is_rebase_in_progress()`". The plan says to clarify that **entire rebase state validation is skipped** (not just tracking check) when a rebase is in progress — meaning the command validates `is_rebase_in_progress()` but **skips all other precondition checks** (like tracking check) because in-progress rebases may have detached HEAD.
Looking at `resolve_conflicts_cmd.py:60-64`, the command simply checks `is_rebase_in_progress()` and errors if false. That's all the validation. The "tracking check" bypass context from the original plan likely refers to the fact that this command doesn't do branch tracking verification — it just checks if a rebase is happening.
Let me add a clarification note to the doc:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
### 3. In-Progress Rebase Path
Detects an existing rebase in progress via `is_rebase_in_progress()`. Displays "Rebase in progress" message and falls through to confirmation.

### 3. In-Progress Rebase Path
Detects an existing rebase in progress via `is_rebase_in_p...[truncated 422 chars]...d checks unreliable. The sole precondition is confirming a rebase is actually in progress.
